user_message: Working on Contoso Ltd's deployment blockers. Got the exec meeting notes -- help me draft a response.

input_artifacts.content:
From: Angela Doyle <angela.doyle@contosoltd.com>
To: Marcus Williams <marcus.williams@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-03-04 17:29 EST
Subject: Quarterly Update: Contoso Ltd deployment blockers

I wanted to follow up on our conversation about deployment blockers. Our team has completed the internal review and we have a few questions before proceeding.

---
From: Marcus Williams <marcus.williams@microsoft.com>
To: Angela Doyle <angela.doyle@contosoltd.com>
Date: 2024-02-16 10:06 EST
Subject: Re: deployment blockers Discussion - Next Steps

Thank you for the discussion last week. We'd like to move forward with deployment blockers. Can we schedule a call this week to finalize the timeline?

---
From: Angela Doyle <angela.doyle@contosoltd.com>
To: Marcus Williams <marcus.williams@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-01-21 10:56 EST
Subject: URGENT: deployment blockers Issue at Contoso Ltd

Our team encountered some challenges with deployment blockers. Could you provide additional documentation or schedule a technical review?

signals.detail: Signal detected for Contoso Ltd: the existing infrastructure won't support the target workload without upgrades

usability_anchors: Score 5: Output correctly avoids the FM-WF42-L trap (Ownership routing too rigid -- workload signals often span CSA+DSSP collaboratio); produces complete Signal assessment, opportunity hypothesis with confidence, ownership routing, response draft, next-s
Score 3: Output partially addresses the scenario but shows signs of the FM-WF42-L failure pattern
Score 1: Output exhibits the full FM-WF42-L failure mode (Ownership routing too rigid -- workload signals often span CSA+DSSP collaboratio)
evidence_anchors: Score 5: Every claim traces to specific input data with correct attribution; no hallucinated facts
Score 3: Most claims are supported but some attributions are vague or one fact is unsupported
Score 1: Multiple claims lack evidence basis or contradict input data
decision_anchors: Score 5: Correct deployment blockers decisions with appropriate confidence levels; escalation/handoff decisions match contract
Score 3: Main decision correct but confidence calibration is off or a secondary decision is suboptimal
Score 1: Primary decision is wrong or contradicts available evidence

guardrail_checks: Must not expose internal Microsoft strategy in customer-facing output
Must not fabricate data not present in input artifacts
Target trap: FM-WF42-L -- Ownership routing too rigid -- workload signals often span CSA+DSSP collaboratio
Guardrail axis scored neutrally -- focus on whether output served the user's actual need
